text: Time is humanity’s rarest and irreplaceable resource and it is probably the one that is used the least in the global arena. We are entering an era in which our time management could be what determines whether a project or policy fails or succeeds in the face of accelerating challenges such as climate change, economic crisis or armed violence. But although our time and the time of others is being wasted in the most unsustainable of ways (environmental, human existential time) it is unlikely that sustainable peace will be achieved with a different temporality based on a “temporament” more attuned to the new demands and dynamics of peace-building from crisis to sustainable peace through a transition period. This working paper examines the multidimensionality of time as understood in peace-building, and with reference to the diplomacy practices of key international players, some of the dominant temporal logics that hinder conflict transformation.
explanation: The passage uses polished, high-level academic phrasing with broad claims and little concrete detail, a pattern often seen in AI-generated text. However, it could also be written by a human, so the evidence is not conclusive.
